title: Introduction to the cerebroApp workflow (Seurat)
text: Now, create Seurat object filter cells less 50 transcripts fewer 10 expressed genes. cut-offs reasonable example data set likely need adjusted real data set. , follow standard Seurat workflow, including… log-normalization transcript counts, identification highly variably genes, scaling expression values regression number transcripts per cell, principal component analysis (PCA).
code: seurat <- CreateSeuratObject(   project = 'pbmc',   counts = pbmc,   min.cells = 5 ) seurat <- subset(seurat, subset = nCount_RNA >= 50 & nFeature_RNA >= 10) seurat <- NormalizeData(seurat, verbose = FALSE) seurat <- FindVariableFeatures(seurat, verbose = FALSE) seurat <- ScaleData(seurat, vars.to.regress = 'nCount_RNA', verbose = FALSE) seurat <- RunPCA(seurat, features = seurat@assays$RNA@var.features, verbose = FALSE)

title: Create expression matrix in h5 format
text: vignette, explain can save expression matrix data set .h5/TENxMatrix format use Cerebro. Using .h5/TENxMatrix format results low memory footprint application expression values specific genes directly read disk instead load entire matrix memory. Moreover, depending size, time takes load data set Cerebro may greatly reduced meta data tables must loaded, usually small compared expression matrix. catch: Reading data disk tends slower reading memory, always noticeable may compromise worth making.

title: Create expression matrix in h5 format
text: Make sure Matrix, HDF5Array cerebroApp packages installed. example, store output temporary directory. expression matrix look similar :
code: if ( 'Matrix' %in% installed.packages() == FALSE ) install.packages('Matrix') if ( 'HDF5Array' %in% installed.packages() == FALSE ) install.packages('HDF5Array') if ( 'cerebroApp' %in% installed.packages() == FALSE ) install.packages('cerebroApp')  library(HDF5Array) library(HDF5Array) output_dir <- tempdir() ```# Load existing `.crb` file  Assuming that you have already exported your data set to a `.crb` file using the `exportFromSeurat()` or `exportFromSCE()` functions, we are first going to load that file. Here, we use the example data set in the `cerebroApp` package: crb_input <- system.file('extdata', 'v1.4', 'example.crb', package = 'CerebroNexus') crb <- readRDS(crb_input) str(crb$expression)

title: Create expression matrix in h5 format
text: Instead storing matrix current (conventional) format rows representing genes columns representing cells, first transpose . means, rows (genes) become columns, columns (cells) become rows. way matrix indexed greatly favors extraction columns rather rows. Cerebro, extract expression values genes, rotate matrix. Importantly, Cerebro expects find matrix expression group .h5 file, make sure specify call HDF5Array::writeTENxMatrix() function. Creating connection file easy : Looks similar matrix started .

title: Export a data set in SCE format
text: example workflow, load small transcript count table Seurat package containing 80 cells 230 genes. want try workflow real data set, can find transcript count matrices Cerebro GitHub repo.

title: Export a data set in SCE format
text: Now, create SingleCellExperiment object filter cells less 50 transcripts fewer 10 expressed genes. cut-offs reasonable example data set likely need adjusted real data set. , … remove genes expressed less 5 cells, log-normalize transcript counts, identify highly variably genes, perform principal component analysis (PCA).
code: sce <- SingleCellExperiment::SingleCellExperiment(   assays = list(counts = pbmc_counts) )  qc_metrics <- perCellQCMetrics(sce) colData(sce) <- cbind(colData(sce), qc_metrics)  cells_to_keep <- sce$sum >= 50 & sce$detected >= 10 sce <- sce[,cells_to_keep]  genes_to_keep <- nexprs(sce, byrow=TRUE) >= 5 sce <- sce[genes_to_keep,]  sce <- logNormCounts(sce)  variable_genes <- modelGeneVar(sce) highly_variable_genes <- getTopHVGs(variable_genes, n = 50)  sce <- runPCA(sce)

title: Export a data set in SCE format
text: , identify clusters using graph-based approach.
code: graph <- buildSNNGraph(sce, use.dimred = "PCA") cluster <- igraph::cluster_walktrap(graph)$membership sce$cluster <- factor(cluster)

code: sce$sample <- factor('pbmc', levels = 'pbmc')  sce@metadata$experiment <- list(   experiment_name = 'pbmc',   organism = 'hg',   date_of_analysis = Sys.Date() )  sce@metadata$parameters <- list(   gene_nomenclature = 'gene_name',   discard_genes_expressed_in_fewer_cells_than = 10,   keep_mitochondrial_genes = TRUE,   variables_to_regress_out = 'nUMI' )  sce@metadata$parameters$filtering <- list(   UMI_min = 50,   UMI_max = Inf,   genes_min = 10,   genes_max = Inf )  sce@metadata$technical_info$cerebroApp_version <- utils::packageVersion('cerebroApp') sce@metadata$technical_info$scran <- utils::packageVersion('scran') sce@metadata$technical_info$scater <- utils::packageVersion('scater') sce@metadata$technical_info <- list(   'R' = capture.output(devtools::session_info()) )

title: Export a data set in SCE format
text: Using SingleR package, can get suggestion cell type cell. Apart suggested cell type, also extract score might help us understand confident assignment . ##Identify marker genes Marker genes genes particularly strongly weakly expressed given cell population, e.g. cluster. information can help distinguish role different cell groups data set. , marker genes identified clusters cell types using findMarkers() function scran package. results separate tables respective subgroups, merge together order make compatible Cerebro.
code: singler_ref <- BlueprintEncodeData()  singler_results_blueprintencode_main <- SingleR(   test = logcounts(sce),   ref = singler_ref,   labels = singler_ref@colData@listData$label.main )  sce$cell_type_singler_blueprintencode_main <- singler_results_blueprintencode_main@listData$labels  singler_scores <- singler_results_blueprintencode_main@listData$scores %>%   as_tibble() %>%   dplyr::mutate(assigned_score = NA)  for ( i in seq_len(nrow(singler_scores)) ) {   singler_scores$assigned_score[i] <- singler_scores[[singler_results_blueprintencode_main@listData$labels[i]]][i] }  sce$cell_type_singler_blueprintencode_main_score <- singler_scores$assigned_score

title: Export a data set in SCE format
code: sce@metadata$marker_genes <- list() sce@metadata$marker_genes$scran <- list()  marker_genes_cluster <- findMarkers(sce, sce$cluster)  for ( i in seq_along(marker_genes_cluster) ) {   marker_genes_cluster[[i]] <- as.data.frame(marker_genes_cluster[[i]]) %>%     mutate(       cluster = names(marker_genes_cluster)[i],       gene = rownames(.)     )   marker_genes_cluster[[i]][[paste0('logFC.', names(marker_genes_cluster)[i])]] <- 0 }  marker_genes_cluster <- do.call(bind_rows, marker_genes_cluster) %>%   select(cluster, gene, Top, p.value, FDR, everything()) %>%   mutate(cluster = factor(cluster, levels = levels(colData(sce)$cluster)))  sce@metadata$marker_genes$scran$cluster <- marker_genes_cluster %>%   filter(!is.na(Top), FDR <= 0.1)  glimpse(marker_genes_cluster)  marker_genes_cluster %>% head() %>% knitr::kable()

title: Export a data set in SCE format
code: marker_genes_cell_type <- findMarkers(sce, sce$cell_type_singler_blueprintencode_main)  for ( i in seq_along(marker_genes_cell_type) ) {   marker_genes_cell_type[[i]] <- as.data.frame(marker_genes_cell_type[[i]]) %>%     mutate(       cell_type_singler_blueprintencode_main = names(marker_genes_cell_type)[i],       gene = rownames(.)     )   marker_genes_cell_type[[i]][[paste0('logFC.', gsub(names(marker_genes_cell_type)[i], pattern = ' |\\-|\\+', replacement = '.'))]] <- 0 }  marker_genes_cell_type <- do.call(bind_rows, marker_genes_cell_type) %>%   select(cell_type_singler_blueprintencode_main, gene, Top, p.value, FDR, everything()) %>%   mutate(cell_type_singler_blueprintencode_main = factor(       cell_type_singler_blueprintencode_main,       levels = unique(cell_type_singler_blueprintencode_main)     )   )  sce@metadata$marker_genes$scran$cell_type_singler_blueprintencode_main <- marker_genes_cell_type %>%   filter(!is.na(Top), FDR <= 0.1)  glimpse(marker_genes_cell_type)  marker_genes_cell_type %>% select(1:6) %>% head() %>% knitr::kable(booktabs = T)

title: Export and visualize custom tables and plots
text: second example, prepare dot plot showing expression significant marker gene cluster. plot ready, can store Seurat object export . Shown first screenshot interactive version plot:  notice legend dot size missing. cases plot isn’t shown correctly (conversion plotly works well always perfectly), can switch interactivity see plain, unmodified plot:
code: library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) library(tidyr)  # cells will be grouped by clusters that they have been assigned to cluster_ids <- levels(seurat@meta.data$seurat_clusters)  # select a set of genes for which we want to show expression genes_to_show <- seurat@misc$marker_genes$cerebro_seurat$seurat_clusters %>%   group_by(seurat_clusters) %>%   arrange(p_val_adj) %>%   slice(1) %>%   pull(gene)  # for every cluster-gene combination, calculate the average expression across # all cells and then transform the data into a data frame expression_levels_per_cluster <- vapply(     cluster_ids, FUN.VALUE = numeric(length(cluster_ids)), function(x) {       cells_in_current_cluster <- which(seurat@meta.data$seurat_cluster == x)       Matrix::rowMeans(seurat@assays$SCT@data[genes_to_show,cells_in_current_cluster])     }   ) %>%   t() %>%   as.data.frame() %>%   mutate(cluster = rownames(.)) %>%   select(cluster, everything()) %>%   pivot_longer(     cols = c(2:ncol(.)),     names_to = 'gene'   ) %>%   rename(expression = value) %>%   mutate(id_to_merge = paste0(cluster, '_', gene))  # for every cluster-gene combination, calculate the percentage of cells in the # respective group that has at least 1 transcript (this means we consider it # as expressing the gene) and then transform the data into a data frame percentage_of_cells_expressing_gene <- vapply(     cluster_ids, FUN.VALUE = numeric(length(cluster_ids)), function(x) {       cells_in_current_cluster <- which(seurat@meta.data$seurat_cluster == x)       Matrix::rowSums(seurat@assays$SCT@data[genes_to_show,cells_in_current_cluster] != 0)     }   ) %>%   t() %>%   as.data.frame() %>%   mutate(cluster = rownames(.)) %>%   select(cluster, everything()) %>%   pivot_longer(     cols = c(2:ncol(.)),     names_to = 'gene'   ) %>%   rename(cell_count = value) %>%   left_join(     .,     seurat@meta.data %>%       group_by(seurat_clusters) %>%       tally() %>%       rename(cluster = seurat_clusters),     by = 'cluster') %>%   mutate(     id_to_merge = paste0(cluster, '_', gene),     percent_cells = cell_count / n   )  # merge the two data frames created before and plot the data custom_plot <- left_join(     expression_levels_per_cluster,     percentage_of_cells_expressing_gene %>% select(id_to_merge, percent_cells),     by = 'id_to_merge'   ) %>%   mutate(     cluster = factor(cluster, levels = rev(cluster_ids)),     gene = factor(gene, levels = genes_to_show)   ) %>%   ggplot(aes(gene, cluster)) +   geom_point(aes(color = expression, size = percent_cells)) +   scale_color_distiller(     palette = 'Reds',     direction = 1,     name = 'Log-normalised\nexpression',     guide = guide_colorbar(frame.colour = "black", ticks.colour = "black")   ) +   scale_size(name = 'Percent\nof cells', labels = scales::percent) +   labs(y = 'Cluster', color = 'Expression') +   coord_fixed() +   theme_bw() +   theme(     axis.title.x = element_blank(),     axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)   ) seurat@misc$extra_material$plots <- list(   "dot_plot_marker_genes_clusters" = custom_plot )  exportFromSeurat(   seurat,   assay = 'SCT',   slot = 'data',   file = '~/Dropbox/Cerebro_development/pbmc_Seurat.crb',   experiment_name = 'pbmc_Seurat',   organism = 'hg',   groups = c('sample','seurat_clusters','cell_type_singler_blueprintencode_main'),   cell_cycle = c('cell_cycle_seurat'),   nUMI = 'nCount_RNA',   nGene = 'nFeature_RNA',   add_all_meta_data = TRUE,   verbose = FALSE ) [10:03:13] Start collecting data... [10:03:14] Overview of Cerebro object:  class: Cerebro_v1.3 cerebroApp version: 1.3.0 experiment name: pbmc_Seurat organism: hg date of analysis: 2020-02-19 date of export: 2020-09-25 number of cells: 5,697 number of genes: 15,907 grouping variables (3): sample, seurat_clusters, cell_type_singler_blueprintencode_main cell cycle variables (1): cell_cycle_seurat projections (2): UMAP, UMAP_3D trees (3): sample, seurat_clusters, cell_type_singler_blueprintencode_main most expressed genes: sample, seurat_clusters, cell_type_singler_blueprintencode_main marker genes:   - cerebro_seurat (3): sample, seurat_clusters, cell_type_singler_blueprintencode_main enriched pathways:   - cerebro_seurat_enrichr (3): sample, seurat_clusters, cell_type_singler_blueprintencode_main,    - cerebro_GSVA (3): sample, seurat_clusters, cell_type_singler_blueprintencode_main trajectories:   - monocle2 (1): highly_variable_genes extra material:   - plots (1): dot_plot_marker_genes_clusters  [10:03:14] Saving Cerebro object to: ~/Dropbox/Cerebro_development/pbmc_Seurat.crb [10:03:18] Done!

title: HLA Associations on bulk TCRβ with real donor HLA
text: Bulk data Seurat object, assemble Cerebro object hand. essence short: declare contracts, store 0-gene matrix, add receptors real HLA. rest guide fills meta, ir, hla_long, shows one.
code: # ... build `meta` (one row per analysis unit), `ir` (receptors), `hla_long` (genotypes)  crb <- Cerebro_v1.3$new() crb$technical_info <- list(observation_unit = "analysis unit",   # a row is NOT a cell                            receptor_key     = "v_gene+cdr3",                            tcr_selection    = "association-conditioned") crb$expression <- Matrix::Matrix(0, nrow = 0, ncol = nrow(meta), sparse = TRUE)  # no transcriptome crb$meta_data  <- meta crb$addImmuneRepertoire(ir)                                # real receptors crb$addHLATyping(hla_long, source_type = "genotyped")      # real genotypes saveRDS(crb, "demo_hla_tcr_bulk_toy.crb")  launchCerebro(crb_file_to_load = "demo_hla_tcr_bulk_toy.crb")

code: a02_carriers <- donors[1:15] hla_long <- do.call(rbind, lapply(donors, function(d) {   a1 <- if (d %in% a02_carriers) "A*02:01" else sample(c("A*01:01", "A*03:01", "A*11:01"), 1)   a2 <- sample(c("A*01:01", "A*03:01", "A*24:02", "A*11:01"), 1)   data.frame(     sample = d, donor_id = d,     locus = c("HLA-A", "HLA-A", "HLA-B", "HLA-B"),     copy  = c(1L, 2L, 1L, 2L),     allele = c(a1, a2, sample(c("B*07:02", "B*08:01"), 1), sample(c("B*44:02", "B*35:01"), 1)),     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ) })) head(hla_long, 6) #>     sample  donor_id locus copy  allele #>  donor_001 donor_001 HLA-A    1 A*02:01 #>  donor_001 donor_001 HLA-A    2 A*03:01 #>  donor_001 donor_001 HLA-B    1 B*07:02 #>  donor_001 donor_001 HLA-B    2 B*44:02 #>  donor_002 donor_002 HLA-A    1 A*02:01 #>  donor_002 donor_002 HLA-A    2 A*11:01

what: Clonotypes: association-conditioned receptors over a background
title: HLA Associations on bulk TCRβ with real donor HLA
text: Six “public” receptors placed carriers almost non-carrier — designed-contrast positive-control warning . Everyone also gets handful private background clonotypes. Look built — one donor’s receptor table. Note CTgene holds V family (TRBV27), CTnt / CTstrict NA: J gene bulk immunoSEQ report one — parser simply leaves j_gene NA. .crb declare receptor_key = "v_gene+cdr3": receptor identified V family plus CDR3, CDR3 alone.
code: assoc_tcrs <- replicate(6, list(v = paste0("TRBV", sample(2:29, 1)), cdr3 = rand_cdr3()), simplify = FALSE)  ir <- list(); meta_rows <- list() for (d in donors) {   clones <- replicate(sample(8:15, 1),                       list(v = paste0("TRBV", sample(2:29, 1)), cdr3 = rand_cdr3()),                       simplify = FALSE)   for (a in assoc_tcrs) {                       # associated receptors: ~85% of carriers, ~8% of others     p <- if (d %in% a02_carriers) 0.85 else 0.08     if (runif(1) < p) clones[[length(clones) + 1]] <- a   }   v <- vapply(clones, `[[`, character(1), "v")   cdr3 <- vapply(clones, `[[`, character(1), "cdr3")   bc <- sprintf("%s_%04d", d, seq_along(clones))   ir[[d]] <- data.frame(     barcode = bc, CTgene = v, CTnt = NA_character_,   # V family only; bulk has no J gene     CTaa = cdr3, CTstrict = NA_character_, stringsAsFactors = FALSE   )   meta_rows[[d]] <- data.frame(     cell_barcode = bc, sample = d, donor_id = d,     cell_type = "T cell (bulk TCRb)",                 # a single level: no CD4/CD8 split     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ) } meta <- do.call(rbind, meta_rows) head(ir[["donor_001"]], 4) #>         barcode CTgene CTnt            CTaa CTstrict #>  donor_001_0001 TRBV27 <NA>   CASSKIAMLVNDF     <NA> #>  donor_001_0002 TRBV25 <NA> CASSVCAMHCAGQIF     <NA> #>  donor_001_0003 TRBV16 <NA>    CASSRMEDCDVF     <NA> #>  donor_001_0004 TRBV26 <NA>     CASSIPVSGIF     <NA>

text: Now set object’s slots directly, including declared contracts technical_info: Confirm contracts round-trip — reload read back: Zero genes, declared analysis unit, positive-control flag, real (“genotyped”) HLA — whole bulk story four lines.
code: crb <- Cerebro_v1.3$new() crb$experiment <- list(   experiment_name = "Toy bulk TCRb cohort - synthetic HLA associations",   organism = "hg", date_of_export = format(Sys.Date()) ) crb$parameters <- list()  # the declared contracts the HLA page reads crb$technical_info <- list(   note = "Bulk TCR-beta immunosequencing; no transcriptome, no single cells.",   observation_unit = "analysis unit",      # a row is a (donor, clonotype), not a cell   receptor_key     = "v_gene+cdr3",         # split by V gene; CDR3 alone would fuse receptors   tcr_selection    = "association-conditioned",   tcr_selection_detail = paste(     "Toy positive control: six public receptors were placed preferentially in",     "HLA-A*02:01 carriers, so any carrier/non-carrier contrast is built in."   ) )  # a real 0-gene x N-unit matrix: bulk measures no transcriptome crb$expression <- Matrix::Matrix(0, nrow = 0, ncol = nrow(meta), sparse = TRUE) colnames(crb$expression) <- meta$cell_barcode crb$meta_data <- meta  crb$addImmuneRepertoire(ir) crb$addHLATyping(hla_long, source_type = "genotyped",                  typing_method = "synthetic demo", source_reference = "vignette toy") crb$addGroup("sample", unique(meta$sample)) crb$addGroup("cell_type", unique(meta$cell_type))  saveRDS(crb, "demo_hla_tcr_bulk_toy.crb") x <- readRDS("demo_hla_tcr_bulk_toy.crb") x$technical_info$observation_unit      # what a "row" is x$technical_info$tcr_selection         # the positive-control flag nrow(x$expression)                     # number of genes unique(x$getHLATyping()$source_type)   # provenance of the genotypes #> [1] "analysis unit" #> [1] "association-conditioned" #> [1] 0 #> [1] "genotyped"

what: The two ideas the page is built on
title: HLA & TCR Motifs: from synthetic data to an interactive app
text: motif network T-cell receptors. Every T cell carries receptor whose variable part short amino-acid string called CDR3 — piece actually touches antigen. page draws one dot per unique CDR3 connects two dots CDR3s length differ exactly one amino acid (one-letter difference called Hamming distance 1). connected clump dots motif: little family near-identical receptors. Motifs matter receptors recognise antigen often converge similar CDR3s. HLA context. T cell see antigen floating free; sees peptide held HLA molecule another cell’s surface. HLA molecules person (genotype) shapes receptors can use. page lets colour CDR3 whether comes carriers chosen HLA allele, tabulates motif co-occurs carriers versus non-carriers. One honesty rule, front. Sharing allele receptor hint, never proof. CD8 T cell donor carries HLA-*02:01 might restricted allele — donor six class-alleles cell use . page always says candidate co-occurrence, confirmed restriction, counts donors, cells (two cells one donor two independent pieces evidence). Proving real restriction needs population statistics plus wet-lab pMHC validation, page .

title: HLA & TCR Motifs: from synthetic data to an interactive app
text: invent twelve donors, D01–D12. HLA-*02:01 teaching anchor: give exactly first six donors, later app opens clean 6-carrier / 6-non-carrier split. DRB1*15:01 class-II anchor carried first five. store wide table: one row per donor, two columns per gene (HLA-A_1, HLA-A_2, …), genotyping spreadsheet usually looks. Keeping donor_id column makes app count donor level. Look built — one row per donor, first columns: Notice first four donors carry *02:01 one two HLA-slots — anchor job. Two things trip people : check.names = FALSE mandatory. title: HLA & TCR Motifs: from synthetic data to an interactive app

code: comp <- c(`CD8 T` = 24, `CD4 T` = 20, Treg = 6, B = 6, Mono = 4) # 60 cells / donor meta <- do.call(rbind, lapply(donors, function(d) {   data.frame(sample = d, cell_type = rep(names(comp), comp), stringsAsFactors = FALSE) })) meta$barcode <- sprintf("%s_%04d", meta$sample, seq_len(nrow(meta))) rownames(meta) <- meta$barcode head(meta, 3) table(meta$cell_type) #>          sample cell_type  barcode #> D01_0001    D01     CD8 T D01_0001 #> D01_0002    D01     CD8 T D01_0002 #> D01_0003    D01     CD8 T D01_0003 #> #>     B CD4 T CD8 T  Mono  Treg #>    72   240   288    48    72

what: Step 3 — a minimal expression matrix and Seurat object
title: HLA & TCR Motifs: from synthetic data to an interactive app
text: HLA page needs gene-expression , Cerebro data set expects matrix one 2-D projection, Gene expression tab friendlier real gene symbols searchable. fake Poisson counts, bump cell type’s markers, attach toy UMAP hand — real clustering run required. point seurat ordinary Seurat object 600 genes, 720 cells, umap reduction, sample / cell_type labels — nothing HLA-specific yet.
code: markers <- c("CD8A", "CD8B", "CD3D", "CD4", "IL7R", "FOXP3", "IL2RA",              "MS4A1", "CD79A", "CD14", "LYZ") genes <- c(markers, sprintf("GENE%04d", seq_len(600 - length(markers)))) counts <- matrix(rpois(length(genes) * nrow(meta), lambda = 0.2),                  nrow = length(genes), dimnames = list(genes, meta$barcode))  # lift each cell type's own markers so the types separate in the toy UMAP lift <- list(`CD8 T` = c("CD8A", "CD8B", "CD3D"), `CD4 T` = c("CD4", "IL7R", "CD3D"),              Treg = c("FOXP3", "IL2RA", "CD3D"), B = c("MS4A1", "CD79A"),              Mono = c("CD14", "LYZ")) for (ct in names(lift)) {   cols <- which(meta$cell_type == ct)   counts[lift[[ct]], cols] <- counts[lift[[ct]], cols] +     rpois(length(lift[[ct]]) * length(cols), lambda = 8) } counts <- as(counts, "CsparseMatrix")  seurat <- CreateSeuratObject(counts = counts, meta.data = meta) seurat <- NormalizeData(seurat, verbose = FALSE)  # a toy 2-D embedding: one Gaussian blob per cell type centres <- rbind(`CD8 T` = c(-5, 4), `CD4 T` = c(5, 4), Treg = c(6, -3),                  B = c(-6, -4), Mono = c(0, -6)) emb <- centres[meta$cell_type, ] + matrix(rnorm(nrow(meta) * 2, sd = 0.8), ncol = 2) colnames(emb) <- c("UMAP_1", "UMAP_2"); rownames(emb) <- meta$barcode seurat[["umap"]] <- CreateDimReducObject(embeddings = emb, key = "UMAP_", assay = "RNA")  # exportFromSeurat reads nUMI / nGene columns by default; provide them seurat$nUMI  <- seurat$nCount_RNA seurat$nGene <- seurat$nFeature_RNA

what: Step 4 — the receptors, with motif families designed in
title: HLA & TCR Motifs: from synthetic data to an interactive app
text: heart demo, go slowly. motif family set CDR3s one edit apart. grow one branching walk: start random CDR3, repeatedly pick existing member change single interior letter. new member one edit parent, whole family automatically Hamming-1 connected. See family — one call returns clump near-identical CDR3s (differs earlier one single amino acid, shown bold): (exact letters random, get different sequences — always form one-edit-apart family.) Now place six families onto cells. Families 1–4 live CD8 T cells HLA-*02:01 carriers (class-story); family 5 lives CD4 T cells DRB1*15:01 carriers (class II); family 6 scattered random donors come “Mixed”. family gets V gene page’s split--V option keeps apart. last move pack assignments shape scRepertoire (therefore page) expects. chain cell one underscore-joined slot, within chain gene names dot-joined V.J.C; CDR3 amino acids live parallel CTaa string. every cell single TRB chain: Look built — named list, one table per sample, holding five columns: See page read : CTgene splits V gene (TRBV20-1), CTaa takes CDR3. first two rows family — CASSFIHAHWQQEVF CASSFIFAHWQHFVF couple edits apart — exactly structure motif network draw.
code: AA <- strsplit("ACDEFGHIKLMNPQRSTVWY", "")[[1]]         # the 20 amino acids rand_cdr3 <- function(len) paste0("CASS", paste(sample(AA, len - 5, TRUE), collapse = ""), "F")  make_family <- function(n, len = 15) {   members <- rand_cdr3(len)   while (length(members) < n) {     base <- members[sample.int(length(members), 1)]     # pick an existing member     chars <- strsplit(base, "")[[1]]     pos <- sample(5:(len - 1), 1)                        # choose an interior position     chars[pos] <- sample(setdiff(AA, chars[pos]), 1)     # mutate it     cand <- paste(chars, collapse = "")     if (!cand %in% members) members <- c(members, cand)   }   members } make_family(6) #> CASSQLHKRFKQF F F #> CASSQLHKRFKQF P F   <- last-but-one letter F -> P #> CASS D LHKRFKQFFF   <- position 5 Q -> D #> CASSQLHKRFKQF L F   <- last-but-one letter F -> L #> CASSQLH P RFKQFPF   <- position 8 K -> P #> CASSQLHKRF G QFFF   <- position 11 K -> G fam_spec <- list(   list(size = 12, v = "TRBV20-1", lineage = "CD8 T", donors = donors[1:6]),   list(size = 10, v = "TRBV28",   lineage = "CD8 T", donors = donors[1:6]),   list(size = 9,  v = "TRBV19",   lineage = "CD8 T", donors = donors[1:6]),   list(size = 8,  v = "TRBV7-9",  lineage = "CD8 T", donors = donors[1:6]),   list(size = 8,  v = "TRBV5-1",  lineage = "CD4 T", donors = donors[1:5]),   list(size = 7,  v = "TRBV9",    lineage = "CD4 T", donors = donors[c(3, 7, 9, 11)]) )  # eligible cells (T cells, ~90% receptor coverage), drawn from a per-(donor, lineage) pool t_meta <- meta[meta$cell_type %in% c("CD8 T", "CD4 T", "Treg"), ] t_meta <- t_meta[runif(nrow(t_meta)) < 0.90, ] pool <- split(t_meta$barcode, list(t_meta$sample, t_meta$cell_type), drop = TRUE) take <- function(donor, lineage) {                 # hand out one free cell, then remove it   key <- paste(donor, lineage, sep = ".")   if (is.null(pool[[key]]) || length(pool[[key]]) == 0) return(NA_character_)   bc <- pool[[key]][1]; pool[[key]] <<- pool[[key]][-1]; bc }  assign <- data.frame(barcode = character(), v_gene = character(), cdr3 = character()) for (fs in fam_spec) {   cdr3s <- make_family(fs$size)   for (i in seq_along(cdr3s)) {     d <- fs$donors[(i - 1) %% length(fs$donors) + 1]     bc <- take(d, fs$lineage)     if (!is.na(bc)) assign <- rbind(assign, data.frame(barcode = bc, v_gene = fs$v, cdr3 = cdr3s[i]))   } } # every other T cell with a receptor gets a random singleton — the polyclonal background bg <- setdiff(t_meta$barcode, assign$barcode) assign <- rbind(assign, data.frame(   barcode = bg,   v_gene = sample(sprintf("TRBV%d-1", 2:29), length(bg), TRUE),   cdr3 = vapply(sample(12:17, length(bg), TRUE), rand_cdr3, character(1)) )) tcr <- merge(assign, meta[, c("barcode", "sample")], by = "barcode") tcr$CTgene   <- sprintf("%s.TRBJ2-7.TRBC2", tcr$v_gene) # V.J.C tcr$CTaa     <- tcr$cdr3                                 # the CDR3 amino acids tcr$CTnt     <- NA_character_ tcr$CTstrict <- sprintf("%s;%s", tcr$v_gene, tcr$cdr3)  ir_cols <- c("barcode", "CTgene", "CTnt", "CTaa", "CTstrict") ir_data <- lapply(split(tcr, tcr$sample), function(df) df[, ir_cols, drop = FALSE]) names(ir_data)          # one entry per donor head(ir_data[["D01"]], 3) #> [1] "D01" "D02" "D03" ... "D12" #> #>   barcode                 CTgene CTnt            CTaa                 CTstrict #>  D01_0001 TRBV20-1.TRBJ2-7.TRBC2 <NA> CASSFIHAHWQQEVF TRBV20-1;CASSFIHAHWQQEVF #>  D01_0003 TRBV20-1.TRBJ2-7.TRBC2 <NA> CASSFIFAHWQHFVF TRBV20-1;CASSFIFAHWQHFVF #>  D01_0004   TRBV28.TRBJ2-7.TRBC2 <NA> CASSWMDCEPRHFWF   TRBV28;CASSWMDCEPRHFWF

text: motif network almost empty real data — bug? — expected result, exactly demo’s families invented. real repertoire spread thinly across ~20¹⁴ possible CDR3s, genuine one-edit pairs rare. what: Step 3 — attach to the Seurat object and export
title: Immune Repertoire Analysis in CerebroNexus
text: Build named list data.frames (one element per sample) holding scRepertoire clonotype columns, attach Seurat object’s @misc$immune_repertoire slot, export. exportFromSeurat() picks slot automatically. include receptor types, row-bind T- B-cell clonotypes per sample (cells mutually exclusive — cell TCR BCR): data.frame must contain scRepertoire clonotype columns barcode, CTgene, CTnt, CTaa, CTstrict. barcode values match cell barcodes Seurat object’s metadata. Chains (e.g. TRA/TRB, IGH/IGK/IGL) detected automatically CTgene, grouping variables (sample, condition, cell type, …) read cell metadata runtime — clonotype tables need five columns .
code: library(CerebroNexus)  ir_cols <- c("barcode", "CTgene", "CTnt", "CTaa", "CTstrict") to_df <- function(x) x[, ir_cols, drop = FALSE]  ir_data <- lapply(sample_names, function(s) {   rbind(to_df(combined_tcr[[s]]), to_df(combined_bcr[[s]])) }) names(ir_data) <- sample_names  seurat_object@misc$immune_repertoire <- ir_data  exportFromSeurat(   seurat_object,   file = "my_data.crb",   experiment_name = "my_experiment",   organism = "hg",   groups = c("sample", "seurat_clusters") )

code: ev_dir  <- file.path(BASE, "cell_bc_plots") ev_file <- list.files(file.path(ev_dir, "cells_1_coordinates_assigned"),                       pattern = "\\.jpe?g$", full.names = TRUE) ev_bc   <- paste0(sub("\\.jpe?g$", "", basename(ev_file)), "-1")  encode_jpeg <- function(path, max_px = 620L, quality = 68L) {   img <- magick::image_read(path)   img <- magick::image_resize(img, paste0(max_px, "x", max_px, ">"))   raw <- magick::image_write(img, format = "jpeg", quality = quality)   paste0("data:image/jpeg;base64,", base64enc::base64encode(raw)) }

Embedding whole-transcriptome expression blows sensible size budget (measured: ~3.8 MB 2,500 nuclei, images). “Whole transcriptome” point, keep genes -sample nuclei instead — stratified cluster — force-include 50 nuclei carry evidence image, drill-still works.
code: strat <- integer(0) for (lv in sort(unique(clab))) {   w <- which(clab == lv)   k <- max(1L, round(N_CELLS * length(w) / length(clab)))   strat <- c(strat, sample(w, min(k, length(w)))) } idx    <- sort(unique(c(strat, match(ev_bc, bc_all))))   # + the evidence nuclei sub_bc <- bc_all[idx]

what: Step 6 — export a Cerebro object and attach the trekker slot
title: Trekker single-cell spatial mapping: from a vendor bundle to an interactive app
text: build ordinary Cerebro_v1.3 object exportFromSeurat() (whole- transcriptome expression + UMAP + cluster/celltype groups) — powers gene colouring page. attach trekker slot everything else bespoke page needs. trekker slot holds page’s content. Every vector unname()d — named R vector serialises JSON object (barcode → value), client indexes positionally arrays. barcodes field lets server pull gene’s expression aligned exact points, regardless expression matrix’s internal column order. whole build one command:
code: sub <- subset(so, cells = sub_bc) sub$celltype <- CELLTYPE_BY_CLUSTER[as.integer(as.character(sub$seurat_clusters)) + 1L] sub$cluster  <- factor(as.integer(as.character(sub$seurat_clusters))) sub$nUMI <- sub$nCount_SCT; sub$nGene <- sub$nFeature_SCT sub <- DietSeurat(sub, assays = "SCT", dimreducs = "umap")   # drop dense scale.data  exportFromSeurat(   sub, assay = "SCT", slot = "data",   file = "inst/extdata/v1.4/demo_trekker.crb",   experiment_name = "Trekker mouse brain (TrekkerU_C, demo)",   organism = "mm",   groups = c("cluster", "celltype"), main_group = "celltype",   nUMI = "nUMI", nGene = "nGene" ) trekker <- list(   meta       = list(n_cells = length(idx), n_cells_full = length(bc_all),                     n_genes_obj = nrow(so), unit = "um (per manual; not declared)"),   qc         = qc,                                  # vendor field names (Step 3)   barcodes   = unname(sub_bc),                      # SAME order as the arrays below   x = unname(round(cx[idx], 2)), y = unname(round(cy[idx], 2)),   # canonical µm   ux = unname(round(um[idx, 1], 3)), uy = unname(round(um[idx, 2], 3)),  # UMAP   clusters   = unname(clab[idx]),   celltype   = CELLTYPE_BY_CLUSTER,                 # cluster id -> label   moran      = moran,                               # upstream vendor Moran's I   evidence   = evidence,                            # {cell, bc, img=base64}   qc_examples = qc_examples                         # one image per excluded class )  crb <- readRDS("inst/extdata/v1.4/demo_trekker.crb") crb$addTrekker(trekker) saveRDS(crb, "inst/extdata/v1.4/demo_trekker.crb", compress = "xz") # TREKKER_OUTPUT_DIR=/path/.../output Rscript data-raw/build_trekker_demo.R # -> inst/extdata/v1.4/demo_trekker.crb  (~4.7 MB, 2,532 nuclei x 21,374 genes, #                                         50 embedded evidence images)

title: Interactive visualization of single cell transcriptomics
text: exportFromSeurat() (convertSeuratToCerebro()) accept expression_matrix_mode = c("embedded", "bpcells", "h5") count matrix persisted alongside .crb: Benchmark trade-offs PBMC fixture (38,606 genes × 147,756 cells): Browser-side TTFB / DOM-ready / load within ~10 ms across backends — divergence second row server-side R work (readRDS + .attachExternalExpression()) plus constant ~5 s Shiny session handshake. Disk-size caveat — depends fixture size. 391 MB h5 total large fixture HDF5’s default gzip filter TENx CSC layout compresses sparse counts well (~6× vs uncompressed). small/dense fixtures trade-can flip — example Roman Hillje’s inst/extdata/v1.4/example.h5 (1000 cells × 500 genes) sibling .h5 actually larger equivalent embedded .crb , metadata + chunk overhead dominates compressed payload. h5 win RAM load time consistent across fixture sizes; win disk emerges scale. Since 1.7.0 bpcells exporter automatically calls BPCells::convert_matrix_type("uint32_t") whenever input values losslessly representable non-negative integers (typical scRNA-seq counts case), triggers BPCells’s bit-packed integer storage shrinks sibling ~5× vs raw double; normalised float values (slot = "data" / "scale.data") exporter falls back raw storage avoid silent precision loss. Picking one: h5 (recommended default) — fastest startup, lowest RAM, fastest queries; smallest disk large fixtures (subject caveat ). TENx CSC layout aligns Cerebro reads expression (per-gene = single column slice), HDF5 page-caching makes repeated reads memory-fast without committing whole matrix RAM. Requires HDF5Array Bioconductor package host. bpcells — RAM-constrained host large matrices, workloads dominated chunk-level batched operations rather per-gene reads. Disk size similar h5 integer counts (bit-packed since 1.7.0); per-gene query ~0.7 s, chunk-level batched ops benefit per-gene streaming. embedded — single-file convenience (sibling manage), compatibility old .crb readers. ~14 s end--end pins full matrix RAM per loaded copy. Best small datasets one-shot scripts. reference, 1.7.0 lazy h5 refactor, h5 attach eager (rhdf5::h5read + full dgCMatrix reconstruction), giving ~33 s open-URL time, ~11 GB RSS, ~0.45 s queries — .e. lazy-h5 backend attach ~263× faster, RAM ~10× smaller, queries ~45× faster, web load ~4× faster. createShinyApp() already knows <stem>.bpcells/ <stem>.h5 copies next bundled .crb. Shiny runtime re-resolves sibling location load via getExpressionBackend()$location relative .crb’s parent directory, bundle stays portable.

title: R6 class in which data sets will be stored for visualization in Cerebro. — Cerebro_v1.3
text: version cerebroApp version used create object. experiment list contains meta data data set, including experiment name, species, date export. technical_info list contains technical information analysis, including R session info. parameters list contains important parameters used analysis, e.g. cut-values cell filtering. groups list contains specified grouping variables group levels (subgroups) belong . grouping variable, corresponding column name must exist meta data. cell_cycle vector contains name columns meta data contain cell cycle assignments. gene_lists list contains gene lists, e.g. mitochondrial /ribosomal genes. expression matrix-like object holds transcript counts. expression_backend list describing /expression matrix stored. step 7.1 every newly exported object tags list(type = "embedded", location = NULL); future step 7.2 introduce type = "h5" / "bpcells" external location. Older .crb files (serialised field existed) load expression_backend = NULL; getExpressionBackend() treats "embedded" backward compatibility. meta_data data.frame contains cell meta data. projections list contains projections/dimensional reductions. most_expressed_genes list contains data.frame holding expressed genes grouping variable specified call getMostExpressedGenes. mean_expression list contains data.frame holding mean expression per gene grouping variable. marker_genes list contains list every method used calculate marker genes, data.frame grouping variable, e.g. specified call getMarkerGenes. enriched_pathways list contains list every method used calculate marker genes, data.frame grouping variable, e.g. specified call getEnrichedPathways performGeneSetEnrichmentAnalysis. trees list contains phylogenetic tree (class phylo) grouping variables. trajectories list contains list every method used calculate trajectories, , depending method, data.frame list specific trajectory, e.g. extracted extractMonocleTrajectory. extra_material list can contain additional material related data set; tables stored data.frame format named list called `tables` immune_repertoire list data.frames (one per sample) containing scRepertoire columns (CTgene, CTnt, CTaa, CTstrict, etc.). hla_typing canonical HLA typing data.frame (long: one row per sample x locus x copy) data provenance, NULL. Consumed HLA & TCR Motifs page donor-level HLA context. Optional; older .crb files simply lack . bcr_data list contains BCR data (kept backward compatibility older .crb files). tcr_data list contains TCR data (kept backward compatibility older .crb files). spatial list contains spatial data (coordinates expression). trekker list Trekker single-cell spatial-mapping content: canonical variant (transposed / y-mirrored) coordinates, UMAP coordinates, per-nucleus cluster/cell-type, positioning QC vendor's original field names, upstream (vendor) Moran's table, per-nucleus positioning-evidence images (base64 data: URIs). Consumed Trekker page. Optional; older .crb files simply lack .

title: Convert Seurat Object to Cerebro Format — convertSeuratToCerebro
code: convertSeuratToCerebro(   seurat_file,   result_dir,   assay = "RNA",   slot = "data",   experiment_name = "Dura Mater - All Cells",   organism = "Human",   groups = c("seurat_clusters", "orig.ident", "cell_type_final"),   groups_naming = NULL,   max_group_levels = 100,   nUMI = "nCount_RNA",   nGene = "nFeature_RNA",   add_all_meta_data = TRUE,   use_delayed_array = FALSE,   expression_matrix_mode = c("embedded", "bpcells", "h5"),   verbose = TRUE,   cell_cycle = NULL,   marker_file = NULL,   marker_method = "Diff. Expression",   add_most_expressed_genes = TRUE,   most_expressed_genes = NULL,   bcr_file = NULL,   tcr_file = NULL )

title: Convert Seurat Object to Cerebro Format — convertSeuratToCerebro
text: seurat_file Character string specifying path Seurat object file. Supported format: .rds. result_dir Character string specifying directory Cerebro output file (.crb) saved. assay Character string specifying assay use Seurat object; default: "RNA". slot Character string specifying slot extract expression data (e.g., "data", "counts", "scale.data"); default: "data". experiment_name Character string experiment name stored Cerebro object; default: "Dura Mater - Cells". organism Character string specifying organism (e.g., "Human", "Mouse"); default: "Human". groups Character vector column names Seurat metadata use grouping variables; default: c("seurat_clusters", "orig.ident", "cell_type_final"). groups_naming Named list renaming grouping variables. Names old column names, values new names; default: NULL. max_group_levels Numeric value specifying maximum number unique levels allowed grouping variable. Groups unique values threshold excluded; default: 100. nUMI Character string specifying column name metadata containing number UMIs per cell; default: "nCount_RNA". nGene Character string specifying column name metadata containing number expressed genes per cell; default: "nFeature_RNA". add_all_meta_data Logical indicating whether include metadata columns export; default: TRUE. use_delayed_array Logical indicating whether convert expression data DelayedArray format memory efficiency; default: FALSE. Ignored expression_matrix_mode set external backend. expression_matrix_mode persist expression matrix generated .crb. One "embedded" (default), "bpcells" "h5". See exportFromSeurat details; briefly, "embedded" stores matrix inside .crb (legacy behaviour), "bpcells" writes -disk BPCells directory next .crb keeps lightweight handle inside (typically reduces .crb size ~80 "h5" writes TENx-format HDF5 file next .crb Shiny runtime loads lazily, minimising RAM startup time (recommended default large datasets). Shiny runtime re-resolves backends relative .crb's parent directory, packaging .crb sibling <stem>.bpcells/ <stem>.h5 together enough portable deployment. verbose Logical indicating whether print progress messages; default: TRUE. cell_cycle Character vector column names metadata containing cell cycle phase assignments; default: NULL. marker_file Character string specifying path marker gene table file. Supported formats: .csv, .tsv, .txt, .tab; default: NULL. marker_method Character string specifying name method used identify marker genes (used label Cerebro); default: "Diff. Expression". add_most_expressed_genes Logical indicating whether calculate expressed genes group; default: TRUE. most_expressed_genes Optional pre-calculated expressed genes data. Can either data.frame (converted list(unknown = ...)) list data.frames. list elements unnamed, assigned names like "unknown1", "unknown2", etc.; default: NULL. bcr_file Character string specifying path BCR data file (.rds format). data merged unified immune_repertoire slot Seurat object export; default: NULL. tcr_file Character string specifying path TCR data file (.rds format). data merged unified immune_repertoire slot Seurat object export; default: NULL.

title: Convert Seurat Object to Cerebro Format — convertSeuratToCerebro
text: function return value. saves Cerebro object (.crb file) specified result_dir.

title: Convert Seurat Object to Cerebro Format — convertSeuratToCerebro

Compatible existing .crb readers. "bpcells" writes matrix BPCells -disk directory   next .crb keeps lightweight handle   serialised object. Recommended large sparse matrices. resulting   .crb portable long sibling .bpcells/ directory   travels ; Shiny runtime re-resolves paths via   getExpressionBackend()$location relative .crb's   parent directory (step 7.3 runtime attach). "h5" writes matrix via HDF5Array::writeTENxMatrix()   TENx-format sparse HDF5 file next .crb (sibling   <stem>.h5) tags backend relative location.   -disk layout matches inst/extdata/v1.4/example.h5: single   /expression group data, indices, indptr,   shape, genes, barcodes datasets. matrix   stored cells x genes (TENx column-favoured, optimised per-gene   reads); Shiny runtime attach reads back lazy   HDF5Array::TENxMatrix seed transposes lazily Cerebro's   internal genes x cells layout via DelayedArray::t() (free).   -memory dgCMatrix never materialised attach, RAM stays   close .crb metadata size. title: Version 2.3.0
text: New page: HLA & TCR Motifs. standalone top-level tab (peer Immune Repertoire) draws CDR3 motif network — every unique CDR3 node edge joins two equal-length CDR3s Hamming distance 1 — alongside donor-level HLA context. appears conditionally, loaded .crb carries TRA/TRB chain. Three sub-tabs: Motif Network (colour motif cluster, cell type, MHC context, HLA carrier status, sample origin), HLA Associations (descriptive carrier vs. non-carrier overlap — p-value, restriction claim), Data & QC (coverage, normalized typing, session-HLA upload). HLA typing data class. Cerebro_v1.3 gained optional hla_typing slot addHLATyping() / getHLATyping(); getter backward-compatible older .crb files. Typing accepts canonical long table, wide sample + HLA-*_1/_2 table, named list, carries provenance (genotyped / imputed / synthetic / unknown) synthetic imputed genotype never treated directly typed. Export picks automatically. exportFromSeurat() now reads object@misc$hla_typing (object@misc$hla_typing_source_type), parallel existing immune_repertoire slot. Declared contracts bulk data. .crb may declare observation_unit, receptor_key, tcr_selection technical_info, page states honestly rows analysis units rather cells, receptor keyed V gene + CDR3, carrier contrast positive control rather independent evidence. One demo data set. demo_hla_tcr_dextramer.crb: 12,000 real CD8+ T cells, every one paired αβ clonotype, plus donors’ published HLA genotypes, 10x Genomics’ dextramer cohort (Zhang et al., Sci Adv 2021, CC-). repertoire antigen-selected, makes motif network legible measured sequences, unselected repertoire gives handful disconnected dots. Class (sorted CD8+), Class × Class II pair scope stays hidden demo appears data set carries Class II typing plus lineage column. per-cell dextramer_* columns 10x’s raw binder calls reagent, validated peptide specificity: staining heavily cross-reactive , restriction_in_genotype (yes / / unknown) column ships beside visible app rather vignette. unknown padding: table S1 publishes one HLA-B allele two donors, absence incompletely called locus evidence absence. HLA association contrasts use published genotypes therefore circular — though repertoire still captured reagent panel, ascertainment donor/panel confounding remain, caveat tables now states. Three new vignettes. “HLA & TCR Motifs: synthetic data interactive app” (single-cell, runnable end end), “HLA Associations bulk TCRβ real donor HLA” (bring bulk cohort, positive-control caveat), “Antigen-selected single-cell TCR” (shipped demo’s full download → .crb pipeline).

Physical space transcriptome space, linked. every nucleus spatial UMAP position, two scatterplots shown side side cross-linked: box- lasso-select one pane highlights nuclei , hovering nucleus rings cell panes location reads instantly. toolbar matching app’s plotly modebar (box / lasso select, pan, zoom, reset, download) sits panes. View control switches side--side, single enlarged Spatial-UMAP-pane, Transition view animates nucleus UMAP position physical position. Colour cell type, cluster, whole-transcriptome genes, use Group filters panel (per-grouping pickers projection tabs) restrict view selected cell types clusters. Positioning evidence auditable. Trekker positions inferred, page surfaces vendor’s per-nucleus positioning-evidence images (bead-barcode cloud plus UMI knee plot) sampled nuclei, Cell inspector reports nucleus’s identity together real physical-neighbour cell-type counts within chosen radius (deconvolution estimate). Colour physical map per-cell value — analysis, just view. Beyond cell type / cluster / gene, page colours cross-space metrics computed full positioned set — spatial-neighbourhood purity expression-vs-physical neighbourhood concordance (forms tight anatomical domains vs. dispersed infiltrating, per-cell-type summary honest reminder healthy brain low microglial purity baseline tiling, activation) — numeric per-cell meta column object carries (pseudotime, signature/module score, velocity magnitude, signaling score), existing single-cell analysis result gains physical-space projection page change. demo ships myelination signature score worked example. Trekker can : needs true single-cell identity true physical position nuclei. Canonical coordinates. panes use vendor’s Location CSV, canonical coordinate authority. generic @images slot axis-transposed SPATIAL reduction y-mirrored relative ; vignette documents discrepancy tissue never silently drawn rotated. Honest QC. Positioning QC shown vendor’s field names (missing metric stays missing); “confidently positioned” disclosed including vendor-salvaged multi-location nuclei (labelled vendor_confidently_positioned); values vendor’s reference range flagged without adjudicating sample usability; Moran’s upstream vendor value, labelled never mixed Cerebro’s . Data class. Cerebro_v1.3 gained optional trekker slot addTrekker() / getTrekker(); getter backward-compatible older .crb files predate field. Demo data vignette. real, -sampled demo .crb (demo_trekker.crb, smallest official Trekker bundle: 2,532 nuclei × 21,374 genes, positioning-evidence images embedded) runnable vignette, “Trekker single-cell spatial mapping: vendor bundle interactive app”, covering registration-gated download, bundle contents, reproducible build (data-raw/build_trekker_demo.R).

what: Projection overhaul, unified interface, and cross-tab selection
title: Version 2.1.0
text: Shared projection renderer: Overview, Gene expression, Trajectory Clonal UMAP scatterplots now share one WebGL renderer instead per-tab copies, sizing, legend, hover selection behave consistently across tabs. projection sizes available viewport longer flashes wrong size first paint. Cell selection: box- lasso-select persist across parameter changes, Clear button zoom--selection toggle every projection tab. Hiding group legend also excludes selected-cell counts, plot toolbar (lasso / box-select / zoom / pan / reset / PNG download) available . Interactive Clonal Diversity: Clonal Diversity plot now interactive figure — hovering point shows group’s bootstrap value. Interface: lighter “Console” visual language coloured sidebar icons, one warm palette shared every chart (plotly ggplot), fluid projection layout reclaims space freed removed top bar. floating menu button keeps sidebar reachable phone-sized screens. Render feedback: parameter change dims projection new render flight, sliders debounced dragging longer fires render per step. Fewer empty tabs: Marker genes expressed genes sidebar items appear datasets carry (e.g. hidden spatial demos). Fixes: spatial histology background longer cleared another tab renders; Clonal UMAP host reveals correctly faceting toggled; gene-expression multi-panel selection restored; hidden-group state stays sync server across re-renders; trajectory projection keeps view redraw.

Plot caching: projection hover-info, groups trajectory expression-metric violins, groups composition bar/Sankey plot, Moran’s score now cached per dataset (session-scoped, invalidated dataset switch), switching genes re-rendering longer recomputes . pseudotime plot selects scattergl -front instead converting whole figure afterwards. title: Version 1.7.0
text: External HDF5 expression backend, symmetric bpcells backend: exportFromSeurat() expression_matrix_mode = "h5" writes matrix via HDF5Array::writeTENxMatrix() TENx-format .h5 next .crb. runtime attach loads back lazy HDF5Array::TENxMatrix seed transposes via DelayedArray::t() (free); -memory dgCMatrix never materialised, RAM stays close .crb metadata size attach effectively instant Introduced createShinyApp() bundling self-contained Shiny app one .crb files createShinyApp() now copies <stem>.h5 sibling alongside .crb app bundling, mirroring existing .bpcells/ handling Legacy .crb files (predating expression_backend field) auto-tagged h5 host app sets Cerebro.options[["expression_matrix_h5"]], finally giving inst/extdata/v1.4/example.h5 runtime consumer convertSeuratToCerebro() accepts -memory Seurat object alongside .rds path; output basename derives experiment_name path given createShinyApp() opens ... passthrough callers can forward extra options without signature churn exportFromSeurat() expression_matrix_mode = "bpcells" now auto-detects losslessly-integer values calls BPCells::convert_matrix_type("uint32_t") write_matrix_dir(), triggers BPCells’s bit-packed integer storage typical scRNA-seq counts case. Shrinks bpcells sibling ~5× integer counts (e.g. 50k cells × 20k genes: 440 MB raw double → 78 MB bit-packed; PBMC Samples 38,606 × 147,756: 2.6 GB → 549 MB), queries get ~1.5-1.7× faster side effect (smaller payload read). Normalised float values (slot = "data"/"scale.data") fall back raw double avoid silent precision loss
